lks2.objective: Throw the tennis ball at the mega ball to drive it across the opponent's end line — develop throwing power and retrieval speed.
successCriteria: Throws hit the mega ball regularly
Returns to the throwing line quickly after retrieval
Adjusts power based on where the mega ball is

uks2.objective: Combine throwing accuracy, team coordination and tactical aim — choose over- or under-arm throws based on the mega ball's position.
successCriteria: Aims at the side of the mega ball away from the end line
Communicates with teammates on coordinated throws

equipment.item: Cones for end lines and throwing lines

space.description: Rectangular court with two end zones and a central area for the mega ball. Throwing lines mark where each team must throw from.

steps: Split the class into two teams. Each team behind their end line.
Every child has a tennis ball. The mega ball sits in the middle.
Aim: throw the balls to drive the mega ball across the opponent's end line.
Children can go anywhere on the court to collect tennis balls but MUST throw from behind their own end line.
Game ends when the mega ball crosses one end line — that team wins.
questions: What strategies can you come up with as a team?
Where should you aim your throw to make the mega ball move the most?
What determines whether you should throw over- or under-arm?

step.space: Move the end zones further apart (longer throws) or closer together
step.task: Children can only roll the ball at the mega ball (no throws above waist)
step.equipment: Add extra mega balls — multiple targets to attack
step.people: Play 4 ways with 4 teams (N vs S, E vs W)
